title: astragalus lagopinus
intro: Astragalus lagopinus, also known as Milk Vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce stress. The medicinal properties of Astragalus lagopinus include its ability to increase white blood cell count, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health, making it beneficial for conditions such as anemia, arthritis, and heart disease. Astragalus lagopinus can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
